meta.title: United Global Consulting — Study Abroad: Korea, USA, Europe, Singapore
meta.description: We help students from Uzbekistan enroll in leading universities worldwide. Free consultation, university selection, documents, scholarships, visa.

hero.subtitle: We help you enroll in top universities in Korea, USA, Europe, Singapore and beyond. From program selection to visa approval.
hero.bullet_3: Support all the way to visa
hero.trust_line: Working with officially accredited partner universities

about.title: Why United Global Consulting
about.lead: A new-generation education agency in Tashkent. Transparent, no false promises.
about.body: Our job is to walk the full journey with you: from choosing a country and program to preparing documents, securing admission and the visa. We don't do mass submissions — every student is guided individually until they board the plane.
about.point_1_d: Fixed service fees, no hidden commissions
about.point_2_d: Strategy and program tailored to your situation

services.subtitle: Everything a student needs — in one place

services.s9_d: Flights, insurance, housing, first steps in the destination country

process.title: From inquiry to your first day of class
process.subtitle: A transparent 5-step process
process.step_1_d: We review your goals, budget and language level. Suggest 2–3 countries.
process.step_2_d: We shortlist 5–7 universities with a high probability of admission.
process.step_3_d: Translations, motivation letter, CV, recommendations, financial docs.
process.step_4_d: We submit, secure admission, then handle the visa.
process.step_5_d: Flights, insurance, housing. We stay in touch after arrival.

scholarships.title: Scholarships and grants
scholarships.subtitle: We help apply to programs that cover tuition fully or partially
scholarships.t1_d: 30–70% tuition discount. Popular: GKS, DAAD, Erasmus.
scholarships.t4_d: Grants based on interview, portfolio or test results.

faq.title: Questions and answers
faq.q1: How much do your services cost?
faq.a1: The first consultation is free. Support cost depends on the package and country. All prices are discussed before contract signing — no hidden fees.
faq.q2: Do you work with students without English?
faq.a2: Yes. We offer programs taught in Russian (CIS countries), Korean/Japanese (with motivation to learn), or programs with language preparation.
faq.a3: Yes. The most realistic full-scholarship routes: GKS (Korea), DAAD (Germany), MEXT (Japan), university full-rides in Turkey, Malaysia, China.
faq.q4: How long does the process take?
faq.a4: 3 to 9 months depending on country and deadlines. We recommend starting 6–12 months before your planned entry date.
faq.q5: What if my visa is refused?
faq.a5: We prepare documents thoroughly to minimize refusal risk. If refused, we analyze reasons and help with reapplication (usually possible after 2–6 months).
faq.q6: Can I combine studies with work abroad?
faq.a6: In most countries — yes, a student visa allows 20 hours of work per week (Korea, USA, Germany, Singapore, Malaysia). Full 40h during vacations.
faq.q7: Do you stay in touch after admission?
faq.a7: Yes. We support you for at least 6 months after arrival — bank account, registration, paperwork in the country.
